title: FEMS outcome feedback loop: pack-outcome attribution + scoring tune (folded WP-1-FEMS-Outcome-Feedback-Loop)
goal: After a MemoryCapsule is used in a model call, track the downstream outcome (MT PASS/FAIL, validator verdict, escalation) and feed it back into per-item memory scoring. Good outcomes boost items in that capsule; bad outcomes penalize them. Folded from WP-1-FEMS-Outcome-Feedback-Loop. Ports the Cognee self-improving memory pattern. No LLM required (per FEMS V1 pattern).
expected_diff_shape: Module src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/outcome_feedback.rs implements OutcomeFeedbackLoop, CapsuleOutcome, OutcomeScoringTuner, OutcomeAttribution, and catalog-backed outcome attach submission helpers. Tests live in src/backend/handshake_core/tests/outcome_feedback_tests.rs. Production wiring adds kernel.memory_capsule.attach_outcome to src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/action_catalog.rs and implements OutcomeAttachSubmitter for PostgresKernelActionSubmitter in src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/persistence_postgres.rs. The outcome is persisted as a sidecar MemoryBox/kernel_event_ledger event because the pre-existing CapsuleRecord outcome enum cannot represent MT-158's detailed Pass/Fail/Escalation shape.
unblock_conditions: MT-145 (CapsuleRecorder so outcomes can attach to records) + MT-157 (bitemporal indexing so outcome attribution is replay-aware).
implementation_notes: Per WP-1-FEMS-Outcome-Feedback-Loop preserved_intent. CapsuleOutcome enum supports Pass { mt_id, validator_verdict_id }, Fail { mt_id, validator_verdict_id, failure_class }, Escalation { mt_id, escalation_reason }, and Skipped { reason }. OutcomeFeedbackLoop::record_outcome now takes the score map, pack item refs, and TuningParams; it attaches the outcome first and invokes OutcomeScoringTuner only after a successful attach. Failed attach returns a typed error and leaves scores unchanged. OutcomeScoringTuner remains pure arithmetic and caps the total per-call score delta, including decay. Production attachment is a sidecar MemoryBox/kernel_event_ledger event through KernelActionCatalogV1 action kernel.memory_capsule.attach_outcome, not an in-place CapsuleRecord mutation, because the existing CapsuleRecord outcome enum is narrower than MT-158's detailed outcome shape. PostgresKernelActionSubmitter implements OutcomeAttachSubmitter and the live Postgres test verifies the persisted catalog action payload.
risk_if_missed: Without outcome feedback the scoring formula is frozen at static weights and a memory that consistently leads to failures keeps getting injected.
validator_focus: Pure arithmetic tuner (no LLM). Score changes are bounded per call. Outcome attaches via canonical action catalog.

proof.note: Focused outcome feedback check passed with POSTGRES_TEST_URL.

out_of_original_owned_files.why: Required to register canonical KernelActionCatalogV1 action kernel.memory_capsule.attach_outcome.
Required to provide production OutcomeAttachSubmitter wiring through PostgresKernelActionSubmitter and to harden idempotent duplicate submissions.
Required to update adjacent D.3 integration coverage for the attach-then-tune record_outcome contract.
Required to make existing live Postgres submitter tests compatible with the submitter's blocking runtime requirement and verify adjacent idempotency hardening.
Required to register the contract-named outcome_feedback_tests target.

review.summary: Prior findings on record_outcome tuning, production Postgres attachment, sidecar semantics, and non-mock coverage were verified closed.

static_checks: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check passed for outcome_feedback.rs, outcome_feedback_tests.rs, and memory_persistence_postgres_tests.rs.
rg found no SQLite/OpenAI/Anthropic references in MT-158 touched outcome and persistence files.
rg found no trailing whitespace in MT-158 touched outcome and persistence files.
git diff --check passed with only pre-existing CRLF warnings.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: A failed outcome attach could mutate scores without an audit trail.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: record_outcome attaches first and tunes only after attach_outcome succeeds; a negative test proves failed attach leaves scores unchanged.
PostgresKernelActionSubmitter implements OutcomeAttachSubmitter and live Postgres tests assert the persisted kernel_event_ledger payload.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: A single call could over-penalize memory scores through penalty plus decay.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: The tuner caps total per-call delta after combining outcome adjustment and decay.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Duplicate semantic submissions could fail with idempotency conflicts due volatile receipt fields.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: PostgresKernelActionSubmitter now treats same-key same-semantic capsule submissions as idempotent success and still rejects divergent same-key payloads.

residual_risks: Sidecar outcome events are explicit and queryable in kernel_event_ledger; downstream readers that need joined CapsuleRecord+Outcome views must consume the sidecar event shape in their own MT scope.

minimum_controls: Tuner is pure arithmetic; no LLM call (preserves FEMS V1 no-LLM invariant).
Score changes are bounded per call so a single bad outcome cannot collapse a memory's score.
Outcome attachment flows through KernelActionCatalogV1 so audit trail is complete.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Outcome feedback loop is pure arithmetic, attaches first then tunes, caps per-call delta after combining decay + outcome, skips pinned items, and routes attach through KernelActionCatalogV1 sidecar events. Failed attach returns typed error without mutating scores.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Outcome feedback loop is pure arithmetic, attaches first then tunes, caps per-call delta after combining decay + outcome, skips pinned items, and routes attach through KernelActionCatalogV1 sidecar events. Failed attach returns typed error without mutating scores.
